events.headline: Mauritius weather bulletin – 25 March 2026
events.summary: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 11h30 on Wednesday 25 March 2026.General situation:A light and warm airstream is flowing over the region.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Clouds development are expected this afternoon over the Western part of the island and over the Central plateau with localised showers.These showers may be sometimes of moderate intensity with risks of thunderstorm and may spread in other sectors late in the afternoon.The maximum temperature will be above the mean and will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 34 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.The sky will clear up gradually in the evening and will be mainly fair across the island during the night and tomorrow morning.The minimum temperature will be between 20 and 22 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 24 and 26 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Wind from the Eastern sector at about 15 km/h, becoming variable during the night.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with Southwesterly swells of the order of 2 metres.Practical advice:The public is advised to stay in safe places and avoid open areas, hikings, sea ventures and sheltering under trees in case of thunderstorms.High tides :22h11 and tomorrow 22h57.Low tides :12h28 and tomorrow 15h37.Sunset :18h17.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h15.

events.headline: Meta told to pay $375m for misleading users over child safety
events.summary: [BBC News] The owner of Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp has been found liable by a court in New Mexico. [France 24] A jury in the US state of New Mexico determined on March 25, that Meta misled consumers over safety and enabled harm against users especially children, hiding what it knew about child sexual exploitation on its social media platforms. The company which owns WhatsApp, Instagram and Facebook must pay a penalty of $375 million. The landmark decision comes after a nearly seven-week trial. A Meta spokesperson said the company disagrees with the verdict and will appeal. [France 24] A New Mexico jury on Tuesday found social media giant Meta liable for making children vulnerable to predators and other online dangers across its platforms, marking one of the first major jury rulings on child safety in the social media era. [Al Jazeera] The verdict marks the first time a US state has successfully sued the social media giant over child safety issues.

events.headline: Inside the Palestinian camp in Beirut where residents are staying put, for now
events.summary: A FRANCE 24 team visited the Palestinian camp of Bourj el-Barajneh in the heart of Beirut’s southern suburbs, which are frequently struck by Israeli airstrikes. Yet here, many people say they have little choice but to stay. Bourj el-Barajneh was established in 1948 after Palestinians were forced to leave their homes in what became known as the Nakba (‘catastrophe’). Palestinian refugee camps in Lebanon have evolved from tent settlements into permanent but deprived neighbourhoods.

events.headline: Russia launches 948 drones at Ukraine in largest attack over 24-hour period
events.summary: [BBC News] Cities across Ukraine were hit, with local officials reporting at least eight people killed and dozens injured. [France 24] Moscow fired nearly 1,000 drones at Ukraine in 24 hours, as it starts is spring offensive. One damaged an energy facility on the region of Chernihiv, leaving close to 150,000 consumers in the city and its surroundings without power. Russia's air defences also downed nearly 400 incoming Ukrainian drones, making it the largest reported overnight attack on Russian regions and Crimea since the start of the invasion. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze tells us more from the ground. [France 24] A drone coming from Russian airspace struck a power plant in Estonia on Wednesday, while another crashed into Latvian territory, with officials in Riga saying it was likely a Ukrainian drone gone astray. Kyiv earlier launched a massive drone barrage against Russia, sparking a fire at a major petroleum export hub not far from the Estonian border.

events.headline: In Lebanon, some schools choose to stay open despite the war
events.summary: In Lebanon, despite ongoing strikes and insecurity, some schools have chosen to remain open. FRANCE 24's Serge Berberi and Renée Davis went to meet parents and students - and found some answers.

events.headline: US to deploy elite army brigade to Middle East: reports
events.summary: [France 24] The United States is planning to send some 3,000 soldiers from the elite 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East to support operations against Iran, US media reported Tuesday. They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the Middle East by Friday, the latest deadline set by President Donald Trump for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Wall Street Journal. The Guardian Washington bureau chief David Smith reports. [France 24] The United States is ramping up its military presence in the Middle East even more, despite the US having reportedly sent a 15-point peace plan to Tehran. There are plans to send soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division to support operations against Iran, according to US media reports. They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the region by March 25.

events.headline: War in the Middle East: An opportunity for Iran's minorities?
events.summary: Shiite Persians make up 51 percent of Iran's population. Yet communities like the Azeris, Kurds, Balochs and Arabs also number in the millions. Historically, they have often been ignored or mistreated by the regime in Tehran, both under the shah and the Islamic Republic. These ethnic minorities could play a role in the current conflict raging in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's David Gilbert and Florent Marchais report.

events.headline: US sends 15-point peace plan to Iran while deploying troops in the region
events.summary: The Trump administration is ramping up efforts to end the Middle East war. After Trump said talks were ongoing between Washington and Tehran, Pakistani officials said that the White House had sent a 15-point peace plan to Iran - yet Tehran has dismissed the idea of negotiating with the US. Meanwhile, US media reported that at least 1,000 troops are to deploy to the Middle East. Although it could be to put pressure on Tehran, Iran is also preparing for the possibility of US troops on the ground.

events.headline: Agriterra mise sur la canne à sucre pour développer un écosystème durable
events.headline_original: Agriterra mise sur la canne à sucre pour développer un écosystème durable
events.summary: Agriterra, le pôle agriculture du groupe Terra, met en avant un modèle basé sur l’économie circulaire, où chaque composant de la canne à sucre est valorisé pour réduire les déchets et produire des ressources durables. Dans un contexte marqué par la recherche d’alternatives aux énergies fossiles, l’entreprise souligne que la canne à sucre peut être exploitée dans son intégralité. La bagasse et la paille sont transformées en énergie renouvelable, la mélasse est utilisée pour produire de l’alcool i

events.headline: Guerre psychologique et tempête politique
events.summary: Les vents contraires se déchaînent. Alors qu’au Moyen-Orient, c’est l’escalade, avec pour conséquence une flambée du baril de pétrole qui a atteint jusqu’à 113 dollars lundi matin, à Maurice, c’est la crise politique provoquée par la démission de Paul Bérenger et celle de sa fille Joanna qui polarise toute l’attention. events.headline_original: Trafic à l’aéroport SSR : 16,4 kg de drogue saisis, un policier recherché
events.summary: Une opération menée le lundi 23 mars à l’aéroport Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam (SSR) a permis la saisie de 16,4 kilos de drogue, d’une valeur estimée à Rs 51 millions. events.headline: En solidarité à Paul Bérenger : Iqbal Calcateea met fin à son mandat au MSC
events.summary: Dans un geste politique marquant, Iqbal Calcateea a choisi de quitter ses fonctions de chairman du Mauritius Sports Council (MSC). Et ce, afin d’apporter son soutien à Paul Bérenger, récemment démissionnaire de son poste de vice-Premier ministre. Nommé à la tête du MSC par le Conseil des ministres le 17 janvier 2025, il met fin à son mandat après 13 mois, affirmant faire passer ses convictions avant ses responsabilités officielles. events.headline_original: Blanchiment présumé et enrichissement inexpliqué : Yasish Emrith dans le viseur de la FCC
events.summary: Le directeur de Capitay Mauritius Limited, Yasish Emrith, est au centre d’une enquête de la Financial Crimes Commission (FCC) pour enrichissement inexpliqué et blanchiment d’argent à l’international. events.headline: Iran war pushes plastic packaging costs higher
events.summary: Oil prices rose again on Tuesday, a day after a sharp decline, amid confusion over which way the Iran war is going. Brent crude settled well above $100 a barrel. Meanwhile, rising energy costs stemming from the conflict are causing the cost of plastic packaging to skyrocket, which in turn will make everything on supermarket shelves more expensive. But first: the EU and Australia have sealed a landmark free trade agreement that will eliminate tariffs on most goods.

events.headline: Ghana and EU sign landmark defence deal to combat militant Islamist threat
events.summary: [BBC Africa] Ghana is the first African country to make such a deal with the EU, the Ghanaian vice-president says. [France 24] In tonight's edition: Ghana has become the first African country to enter into a landmark security and defence partnership with the European Union. Also, with several African countries heavily reliant on fertiliser imported from the Gulf, there's been a knock-on effect on the continent as the war in Iran chokes traditional supply routes. Plus the death toll from heavy flooding in Kenya reaches 88, with at least 21 counties affected. [Africanews] The agreement, signed in Accra by EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas and Ghanaian Vice President Jane Naana Opoku-Agyemang, is aimed at strengthening cooperation in areas including counterterrorism, intelligence sharing and crisis response [Africanews] Ghana and the European Union on Tuesday signed their first formal defence partnership, as the country presents itself as a regional bulwark against the growing jihadist violence roiling west Africa.
